blogs.index.title: Ancient Science,
blogs.index.subtitle: Modern Insight
blogs.index.description: Journey through the vast ocean of Vedic knowledge. From planetary movements to spiritual remedies, discover the keys to your cosmic existence.
blogs.index.ctaDescription: Our Celestial Library is expanding every week. If you have a specific question about your chart, Navi has the answers ready for you now.
blogs.houses.title: The 12
blogs.houses.description: In Vedic Astrology, the 12 Houses (Bhavas) represent the 12 domains of human life. While planets provide the energy and signs provide the environment, the houses are the actual fields of experience where karma is enacted.
blogs.houses.kendraDesc: Houses 1, 4, 7, and 10 form the pillars of life. They govern self, home, partnerships, and career. Planets placed here have the maximum power to shape your worldly existence and initiate massive action.
blogs.houses.trikonaDesc: Houses 1, 5, and 9 are the houses of Dharma and pure blessing. They represent past life merits (Purva Punya), intelligence, and divine grace. Planets here provide natural luck and spiritual evolution.
blogs.houses.dusthanaDesc: Houses 6, 8, and 12 govern obstacles, transformation, and loss. While challenging for material pursuits, they are essential for burning karma, building resilience, and achieving ultimate spiritual liberation (Moksha).
blogs.houses.upachayaDesc: Houses 3, 6, 10, and 11 represent areas of life that improve over time through conscious effort. Malefic planets (Saturn, Mars, Sun) placed here actually give excellent results as they provide the drive to overcome competition.
blogs.nakshatras.title: Lunar
blogs.nakshatras.subtitle: Mansions
blogs.planets.title: Navagraha
blogs.planets.subtitle: Archetypes
blogs.planets.description: In Vedic Astrology, the Navagraha (Nine Planets) are the cosmic agents of karma. They act as the dynamic forces that trigger events, shape your psychology, and guide the soul's evolutionary journey through time and space.
blogs.planets.karmicDesc: Planets don't just symbolize traits; they actively deliver the fruits of your past actions. Benefics like Jupiter and Venus bring ease and grace, while Malefics like Saturn and Mars enforce necessary, often difficult, lessons.
blogs.planets.avasthasDesc: A planet's power depends on its condition. An exalted planet expresses its highest, purest potential, while a debilitated planet signifies areas where the soul must work harder to overcome innate weaknesses.
blogs.planets.shadowNodesDesc: Rahu and Ketu are not physical masses, but mathematical points where eclipses occur. Despite lacking bodies, these nodes are profound triggers of sudden destiny, obsession, and ultimate spiritual liberation.
blogs.planets.functionalDesc: Beyond natural traits, a planet acts as a friend or foe based on your specific Ascendant (Lagna). The same Saturn that brings hardship to an Aries lagna acts as a supreme benefactor for a Taurus lagna.
blogs.planets.discoverInfluence: Discover how this planet influences your chart
blogs.yogas.title: Planetary
blogs.yogas.subtitle: Yogas
blogs.yogas.description: In Vedic Astrology, a "Yoga" means union. It refers to specific planetary combinations and alignments that lock together to create powerful, destined effects. These cosmic signatures dictate the heights of wealth, power, and spiritual liberation in a chart.
blogs.yogas.rajaDesc: The kings of combinations. Formed when the lords of Kendra (action) houses unite with lords of Trikona (luck) houses. They guarantee status, authority, and massive success when activated.
blogs.yogas.dhanaDesc: Combinations for absolute wealth. When the houses of earnings (2nd) and gains (11th) connect deeply with the houses of destiny (5th, 9th), the native is destined for immense financial prosperity.
blogs.yogas.activationDesc: Yogas lie dormant in a chart until their planetary lords are activated by the Vimshottari Dasha (time period). When the dasha of a Yoga-causing planet begins, the effects manifest drastically in reality.
blogs.yogas.cancellationDesc: Not all bad yogas are permanent. Neechabhanga occurs when a debilitated (weak) planet is supported by strong alignments, cancelling the weakness and often turning massive struggle into great success.
blogs.yogas.activationPathDesc: Primarily triggers during major Dasha / Bhukti periods of the involved planets.
blogs.yogas.discoverCombinations: Discover hidden combinations in your Kundli
blogs.remedies.title: Sacred
blogs.remedies.subtitle: Remedies
blogs.remedies.discoverSpecific: Discover specific alignments needed for your chart

profile.security.pageDescription: Manage your password, active sessions, and account data.
profile.security.changePasswordWarning: Changing your password will automatically log you out of all active sessions, including this one.
profile.security.sessionManagementDescri: Log out from all other devices where your account is currently signed in. This device will remain logged in.
profile.security.dangerZoneDescription: Permanently delete your account and all associated data. This action cannot be undone.
profile.security.deleteAccountModal.desc: This action is permanent and will securely erase all your birth details, generated horoscopes, and preferences. Please confirm with your password to proceed.
profile.security.logoutConfirmDialog.mes: This will sign out your account from all other devices. Your current session will remain active.
profile.security.errors.passwordMinLengt: New password must be at least 10 characters.
profile.security.errors.passwordRequired: Please enter your password to confirm deletion.
profile.security.messages.passwordChange: Password changed successfully. You will be logged out of all devices.
profile.security.messages.logoutAllSucce: Successfully logged out from all other devices.
